modes.name: Empty/trivial texts
modes.description: Empty or single-token texts cause division by zero or undefined behavior in n-gram metrics.
modes.structural_cause: N-gram metrics require ≥n tokens per text; with fewer, the denominator is 0.
modes.detection_criterion: Any text has fewer than n tokens
modes.mitigation: Filter texts with < n tokens before computing metrics. Return NaN with warning.

modes.name: Identical texts
modes.description: All texts identical. D-2 may still be non-zero (from within-text diversity) while Self-BLEU is 1.0.
modes.structural_cause: D-2 measures type-token ratio which can be >0 for a single long text, while Self-BLEU correctly identifies zero diversity between texts.
modes.mitigation: Check for exact/near-exact duplicates before computing metrics. Flag degenerate case.

modes.name: Extreme repetition
modes.description: Texts consisting of a single repeated word. TTR approaches 1/n_tokens while D-2 is near 0.
modes.structural_cause: TTR denominator grows linearly with text length but numerator is bounded by vocabulary size.
modes.detection_criterion: TTR < 0.05 or single unique token dominates >90% of text
modes.mitigation: Use bias-corrected TTR (MSTTR) or entropy-based measures for highly repetitive text.

modes.name: High within-text, low between-text diversity
modes.description: Texts have rich internal vocabulary but are near-identical to each other. D-2 (computed across all texts) may show high diversity while Self-BLEU correctly shows low diversity.
modes.structural_cause: D-2 pools n-grams across all texts, mixing within-text and between-text diversity. Self-BLEU explicitly compares pairs.
modes.detection_criterion: |D-2 rank - Self-BLEU rank| > 50th percentile for normal inputs
modes.mitigation: Report both within-text and between-text diversity separately.

modes.name: Extreme length variation
modes.description: Texts vary enormously in length. Long texts dominate n-gram counts, making short texts invisible to pooled metrics.
modes.structural_cause: N-gram pooling is count-weighted, so a single long text contributes O(L) n-grams while a short text contributes O(1).
modes.mitigation: Normalize by text length or use per-text metrics averaged across texts.

modes.name: Permutation-only diversity
modes.description: Texts use identical vocabulary but in different orders. TTR shows zero diversity while D-2 and Self-BLEU capture n-gram differences.
modes.structural_cause: TTR is a bag-of-words metric (order-invariant), while n-gram metrics are order-sensitive.
modes.mitigation: Use n-gram metrics when word order matters; report TTR alongside for vocabulary diversity.

modes.name: Vocabulary explosion (hapax legomena)
modes.description: Many unique tokens appear only once. D-2 and TTR are inflated by rare tokens that don't indicate meaningful diversity.
modes.structural_cause: D-2 counts distinct n-grams / total n-grams. With many hapax legomena, the ratio approaches 1 regardless of semantic diversity.
modes.detection_criterion: Hapax legomenon ratio > 0.5 (>50% of types occur only once)
modes.mitigation: Apply frequency thresholding (remove hapax legomena) or use entropy with bias correction.

modes.name: Domain vocabulary clustering
modes.description: Texts from different domains create vocabulary clusters. TTR and D-2 show high diversity due to disjoint vocabularies, not meaningful text variation.
modes.structural_cause: Domain-specific jargon creates non-overlapping vocabulary clusters that inflate type-token and n-gram metrics.
modes.detection_criterion: Vocabulary Jaccard similarity between text subsets < 0.2
modes.mitigation: Compute diversity within and between domains separately. Use embedding-based metrics for semantic diversity.

modes.name: Small sample instability
modes.description: With very few texts (n<5), metric estimates are unstable and bootstrap CIs are unreliable.
modes.structural_cause: Shannon entropy bias is O(m/2N) where m is vocabulary size and N is sample size. For small N, this bias dominates.
modes.mitigation: Require minimum sample size. Use bias-corrected entropy (Miller-Madow or NSB). Report sample size alongside metrics.

modes.name: Metric saturation
modes.description: At large sample sizes with fixed vocabulary, D-2 saturates near its maximum. Adding more texts provides diminishing information.
modes.structural_cause: D-2 = distinct_ngrams / total_ngrams. With fixed vocabulary, distinct_ngrams is bounded while total_ngrams grows linearly.
modes.detection_criterion: D-2 change < 0.01 when adding 10% more texts
modes.mitigation: Report sample-size-adjusted metrics. Use entropy (scales logarithmically) instead of ratio-based metrics at scale.

modes.name: Uniform vs Zipfian distribution
modes.description: Uniform token distribution maximizes entropy but may have lower D-2 than Zipfian distribution due to n-gram diversity patterns.
modes.structural_cause: Entropy is maximized by uniform distributions. D-2 depends on n-gram uniqueness, which can be higher when some tokens are rare (creating unique bigrams).
modes.detection_criterion: Rank correlation between Entropy and D-2 < 0 for specific configurations
modes.mitigation: Report both entropy and D-2. Use KL divergence to quantify distribution shape differences.

modes.name: Syntactic diversity without semantic diversity
modes.description: Texts are paraphrases of the same idea. N-gram metrics show high diversity but semantic content is identical.
modes.structural_cause: N-gram metrics operate on surface forms, not meaning. Paraphrases create distinct n-grams while conveying the same information.
modes.detection_criterion: High D-2/TTR but low semantic embedding diversity (EPD)
modes.mitigation: Always pair surface-level metrics with embedding-based semantic diversity (EPD, Vendi Score).

modes.name: Semantic diversity with syntactic template
modes.description: Texts follow a template with slot-filling. Self-BLEU is high (similar structure) but content varies meaningfully.
modes.structural_cause: Self-BLEU measures n-gram overlap between pairs. Templates create high overlap even when filled content differs.
modes.mitigation: Use content-word-only metrics or mask template words before computing diversity.
